reasoning: DP1.00003.001 is the only air temperature product in the downloaded NEON data. It measures triple-aspirated air temperature (TAAT) using three platinum resistance thermometers in a fan-aspirated shield, providing tempTripleMean in degrees Celsius. This directly maps to the GERI target airTemp_mean_degC. The aspirated shield design minimizes radiative heating bias, making it the standard NEON above-canopy air temperature measurement. No competing air temperature products exist in the dataset.
escalation_steps_used: 1. Schema semantics: target is mean air temperature in degC; tempTripleMean is arithmetic mean of three co-located PRTs in Celsius — exact semantic match.
2. No ambiguity — single candidate product, no escalation needed.

alternatives_rejected.reason: DP1.00006.001 is deprecated as of RELEASE-2025. It was split into three successor products: DP1.00044.001 (primary/weighing gauge), DP1.00045.001 (secondary/tipping bucket), and DP1.00046.001 (throughfall). NEON states the new products should be used exclusively. Additionally, DP1.00006.001 contained throughfall (below-canopy) precipitation which does not match the target definition of total bulk precipitation.

DP1.00045.001 is NEON's secondary precipitation measurement using Met One tipping bucket gauges at tower tops. Tipping buckets are less accurate than the DFIR-shielded weighing gauge (DP1.00044.001), particularly during wind and snowfall — wind-induced undercatch can reach 20-80%. NEON's own ATBD designates the weighing gauge as the primary reference method following WMO guidelines. The tipping bucket is the secondary/fallback instrument.

reasoning: Three precipitation products exist in the downloaded NEON data. DP1.00044.001 uses a Belfort AEPG II 600M weighing gauge housed within a DFIR (Double Fence Intercomparison Reference) enclosure — NEON's designated primary, reference-grade bulk precipitation measurement following WMO guidelines. The variable precipBulk (mm) maps directly to the GERI target precip_tot_mm. DP1.00006.001 is deprecated and was superseded by DP1.00044/00045/00046. DP1.00045.001 is the secondary tipping-bucket method with known wind undercatch issues. Selection is based on semantic match to 'total bulk precipitation' and instrument accuracy, not temporal resolution or data volume.
escalation_steps_used: 1. Schema semantics: target is total precipitation in mm; all three candidates provide precipitation in mm, so semantics alone did not disambiguate.

2. Research: Web search confirmed DP1.00006.001 is deprecated (split Oct 2025). NEON ATBD designates DP1.00044.001 weighing gauge with DFIR as primary reference method per WMO standards; DP1.00045.001 tipping bucket is secondary. Weighing gauge provides superior accuracy especially for snow and windy conditions. Sources: NEON observatory blog, NEON.DOC.000898 ATBD, NEON data product documentation.

reasoning: DP1.00094.001 is the only soil water content product in the downloaded NEON data. It measures volumetric soil water content (VSWC) at multiple depths using in-situ soil sensors. VSWCMean provides mean volumetric water content in cm³/cm³ (fraction 0-1), which will need conversion to percent (0-100) per GERI schema rules (multiply by 100). Sensor positions include depth information (vertical indices 501-508 mapping to specific depths), which maps to the GERI target depth column. No competing soil moisture products exist in the dataset.
escalation_steps_used: 1. Schema semantics: target is mean volumetric soil water content in percent (0-100); VSWCMean measures volumetric soil water content in cm³/cm³ — exact semantic match with known unit conversion (×100).
2. No ambiguity — single candidate product, no escalation needed.

reasoning: DP1.00041.001 is the only soil temperature product in the downloaded NEON data. It measures soil temperature at multiple depths (up to 9 depths per soil plot, 5 plots per site) using in-situ temperature sensors. soilTempMean provides arithmetic mean soil temperature in degrees Celsius, which maps directly to the GERI target soilTemp_Mean_degC with no unit conversion needed. Sensor positions encode depth via the vertical index (501-509), preserving per-depth records as required by the schema's depth column. No competing soil temperature products exist in the dataset.
escalation_steps_used: 1. Schema semantics: target is mean soil temperature in degC; soilTempMean is arithmetic mean soil temperature in Celsius — exact semantic match, no conversion needed.
2. No ambiguity — single candidate product, no escalation needed.

reasoning: DP1.00096.001 (Soil physical and chemical properties, Megapit) is the only soil texture product in the downloaded NEON data. The mgp_perbiogeosample table provides sandTotal, siltTotal, and clayTotal as percentage fractions per soil horizon, mapping directly to the GERI target columns sand_percent, clay_percent, silt_percent. Depth information is available via biogeoTopDepth and biogeoBottomDepth columns, mapping to GERI depthMin_m and depthMax_m. This is a one-time static collection from megapits at each NEON terrestrial site, consistent with the GERI schema treating soil texture as a static product. No competing soil texture products exist in the dataset.
escalation_steps_used: 1. Schema semantics: target is sand/clay/silt percentages with depth interval; sandTotal/siltTotal/clayTotal with biogeoTopDepth/biogeoBottomDepth provide exact semantic match.
2. No ambiguity — single candidate product, no escalation needed.
